meta.title: Incorporation of 3H-Uridine into RNA during Cellular Slime Mould Development

abstractHtml: SUMMARY: Dictyostelium discoideum wild-type amoebae incorporate 3H-uridine into RNA at a linear rate during the developmental sequence until the terminal stage of fruiting body construction. However, 3 aggregateless strains perform in similar fashion (except for the final decrease). A sensitive criterion of bacterial contamination, making use of MAK-column chromatography of the labelled RNA, is described.
